statement: The high-block source model satisfies the finite inactive-tail inequality and spectral-projection hypotheses needed by the abstract min-max tail passage theorem.

finiteTailConstantInputStatus.label: finite certified inactive-tail constant
finiteTailConstantInputStatus.reason: The source-inactive constants theorem supplies the finite certified source-model tail bound.

lowMidAbsorptionInputStatus.label: finite low/mid absorption budget
lowMidAbsorptionInputStatus.reason: The same constants theorem verifies epsilon_delta is absorbable by the finite low/mid Schur budget.

spectralProjectionInputStatus.label: high-block spectral projection input
spectralProjectionInputStatus.reason: The high-block spectral theorem identifies the stable active/inactive source split.

abstractMinmaxInputStatus.label: abstract min-max passage input
abstractMinmaxInputStatus.reason: The abstract min-max theorem passes the finite inactive-tail bound through the convergent source model.

highBlockMinmaxTailInputStatus.label: high-block min-max tail passage
highBlockMinmaxTailInputStatus.reason: The finite tail constant, spectral split, and abstract min-max theorem match.

proof: Use the source-inactive constants theorem for the finite inactive-tail quadratic inequality.
Use the high-block spectral projection theorem to identify the active two-dimensional source subspace and its inactive complement.
Apply the abstract min-max tail passage theorem to transport the finite bound to the continuum closed high block.
